title: The Secret Garden of Mrs. Rose
introKo:
이웃집 Rose 부인의 정원 일을 돕는 아주 건전한 이야기.
…표면적으로는요. 단어를 탭하면 속뜻이 보여요.

quiz.q: Rose 부인이 기르는, 마을에서 가장 큰 것은?

normal.text: Spring comes to the village. Your neighbor, Mrs. Rose, loves her garden very much.
People say she grows the biggest eggplants in town. Today, she calls you over the fence.
"My garden is hungry," she says. "I need strong hands. Can you help me this morning?"

options.en: Sure! I'm good with my hands.
Okay, but I've never done this before.
options.reaction: "Don't worry," she smiles. "I will teach you everything. Everyone has a first time."

normal.text: First, you dig a small hole. "Deeper," Mrs. Rose says. "Don't be shy. The seed needs a deep, warm bed."
"Now water it well. A dry garden is a sad garden. Everything works better when the soil is wet."

options.en: Like this? Am I doing it right?
options.reaction: "Perfect. Slow and gentle," she says. "You are a natural."

options.en: How much water is too much?
options.ko: 물이 얼마나 많으면 너무 많은 거예요?
options.reaction: "Oh, honey," she laughs. "In my garden, there is no such thing as too wet."

normal.text: Next, the zucchini. You hold it carefully. It is long and surprisingly heavy.
"What a big one!" Mrs. Rose says. "Size is not everything — but it never hurts."
You pick two round melons and put them in the basket. "Careful," she says. "Squeeze them softly to see if they are ripe."

options.en: Your garden is amazing, Mrs. Rose.
options.reaction: "Thank you, dear. My husband never watered it enough. Now look at it."
"You should! Gardening is like riding a bicycle — once you learn, your body never forgets."

normal.text: You go home with a basket of vegetables. Your body is tired, but your heart is full.
That night, you sleep very well. Gardening, you think, is the best exercise in the world.

hard.text: Spring settles over the village, and no one welcomes it more eagerly than your neighbor, Mrs. Rose, whose garden is her pride and joy.
People around town swear she grows the biggest eggplants for miles. Today she waves you over the fence with a meaningful smile.
"My garden has been terribly neglected," she sighs. "I could use a pair of strong hands. Would you spare me your morning?"

options.en: Sure! I have always been good with my hands.
options.reaction: "Wonderful," she smiles. "Somehow I knew you would be."

options.en: Okay, but I have never done this before.
options.reaction: "Don't you worry," she smiles. "I will walk you through everything. Everyone has a first time."

hard.text: First you dig a hole. "Deeper," Mrs. Rose insists. "Don't be shy about it. The seed deserves a deep, warm bed."
"Now water it generously. A dry garden is a miserable garden. Everything performs better once the soil is properly wet."

options.en: Like this? Am I doing it right?
options.reaction: "Perfect. Slow and gentle," she murmurs. "You are a natural at this."

options.en: How much water would be too much?
options.ko: 물이 얼마나 많으면 너무 많은 걸까요?
options.reaction: "Oh, honey," she laughs. "In my garden, no such thing has ever existed."

hard.text: Next comes the zucchini. You lift it carefully; it is remarkably long and surprisingly heavy in your hands.
"My, what a big one!" Mrs. Rose exclaims. "Size is not everything, of course — though it certainly never hurts."
You gather two round melons and settle them in the basket. "Careful now," she warns. "Give them a gentle squeeze to see whether they are ripe."

options.en: Your garden is truly amazing, Mrs. Rose.
options.reaction: "Thank you, dear. My husband never watered it nearly enough. And now look at it."

options.en: I really should garden more often.
options.reaction: "You should! Gardening is like riding a bicycle — once you learn, the body never forgets."

hard.text: You head home with a basket of vegetables, arms tired but heart full.
That night you sleep wonderfully well. Gardening, you decide, is the finest exercise in the world.
